description:
You are a support-engineer who has just been asked to do a quick “semantic-version-bump & diagnostics capture” for an internal helper tool that lives in the folder

        /home/user/support-tool

    1. A file called /home/user/support-tool/VERSION currently contains a single line with the text

           0.9.7

       No spaces, no additional lines.  
       Your job is to perform a PATCH bump in strict semantic–versioning form, i.e. turn it into **0.9.8** (major.minor.patch).  
       The file must still contain exactly one line and end with a single trailing newline character.

    2. A file called /home/user/support-tool/CHANGELOG.md already exists.  
       Its first line is “# Changelog” and somewhere below that is the marker line

           ## [Unreleased]

       Replace that marker with a properly dated release entry for the new version using the exact markdown template shown below (the automated tests check for the exact spelling / punctuation):

           ## [0.9.8] - YYYY-MM-DD
           - Collected system diagnostics and saved to diagnostics/latest.txt

       •  Replace YYYY-MM-DD with today’s date in ISO-8601 format returned by the command:  
         date "+%Y-%m-%d"

       •  After inserting the new entry, **re-add an empty “## [Unreleased]” section immediately above it** so that future work still has a placeholder.  
       •  Do not modify or delete any of the older historical release notes that may follow.

       When you are finished, the top of CHANGELOG.md must therefore look exactly like this pattern (where the date will be today’s date):

           # Changelog
           ## [Unreleased]

           ## [0.9.8] - 2025-01-15   ← (example date, yours must be dynamic)
           - Collected system diagnostics and saved to diagnostics/latest.txt

    3. Create the directory /home/user/support-tool/diagnostics if it does not already exist (use default user-writable permissions).

    4. Inside that directory create a file named latest.txt that contains five clearly delimited sections, **in the exact order shown below** and with exactly one blank line between sections.  
       Each section header is a Markdown H3 (three ‘#’ symbols and a space).  
       The automated test relies on these headers being present verbatim:

           ### Hostname
           <output of: hostname>

           ### Uptime
           <output of: uptime --pretty>

           ### Disk
           <output of: df -h -x tmpfs -x devtmpfs>

           ### Memory
           <output of: free -m>

           ### Top Processes
           PID CMD %CPU %MEM
           <output of: ps -eo pid,comm,%cpu,%mem --sort=-%cpu | head -n 6>

       Requirements for latest.txt:
       • The file must be overwritten each time; it must not contain any data other than the five sections.  
       • There must be exactly one blank line between sections; no extra blank lines at top or bottom.  
       • The “Top Processes” section’s first non-blank line after the header **must** be the literal column header

             PID CMD %CPU %MEM

    5. Leave all work inside the /home/user/support-tool tree; no outside files should be touched.

    What the automated grader will check:
    • /home/user/support-tool/VERSION contains the single line “0.9.8”.  
    • /home/user/support-tool/CHANGELOG.md starts with the exact three-section pattern described above, with today’s date in ISO-8601 form.  
    • /home/user/support-tool/diagnostics/latest.txt exists and contains the five required “###” headers in the correct order, each separated by exactly one blank line, and the “Top Processes” column header line is present.  
    • All paths and file names are spelled exactly as described.

    Do not simply echo placeholders—run the commands so real diagnostic data is captured.  
    Work entirely with regular user permissions; no sudo is allowed.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts:

    1. /home/user/support-tool/ exists.

    2. /home/user/support-tool/VERSION  
       Contents (1 line, newline-terminated):  
           0.9.7

    3. /home/user/support-tool/CHANGELOG.md  
       The first three lines are:  
           # Changelog  
           ## [Unreleased]  
           (blank line)

    There is no diagnostics/ directory yet.

    Expected final state after successful completion:

    1. /home/user/support-tool/VERSION  
       Contents (1 line, newline-terminated exactly):  
           0.9.8

    2. /home/user/support-tool/CHANGELOG.md – the very top of the file must match the regex (where DATE is the current date when the task is executed):

           ^# Changelog\n## \[Unreleased\]\n\n## \[0\.9\.8\] - \d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\n- Collected system diagnostics and saved to diagnostics/latest\.txt\n

       Everything after that can be whatever originally existed.

    3. /home/user/support-tool/diagnostics/latest.txt must exist.  
       It must contain exactly five Markdown H3 headers in this order:

           ### Hostname
           ### Uptime
           ### Disk
           ### Memory
           ### Top Processes

       and each header must be separated from the next by exactly one blank line (i.e. a single “\n\n”).  
       The Top Processes section must contain a line that matches the literal string:  
           PID CMD %CPU %MEM

    The grader will fail the task if any of these conditions are not met.
